ref: And that's what happened. God made the dome
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ kpaa, aŋ ka wuoluŋ, dɔŋɔ hɛ nyuŋ, dɔŋɔ ma hɛ bʋbʋɔŋ.
translated: If you go to the farm, you will get food, if you do not go, you will not get anything.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bɩra bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ la ŋaa nuhuobiine dɩ ʋ nagɛla, a leŋ dɩ ʋ tɩɩ chenfile-la sɩ hɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ tʋɔŋ arɩ fille-la kala sɩ hɛ wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ pʋsʋ-la kala sɩ hɛ tinteeŋ arɩ tintee-tuuroo kala.”
translated: And then God said, “Let there be lights in the sky to separate the day from the night, and let them serve as signs to mark sacred times, and days and years, and let them be lights in the sky to give light on the earth.”

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Wɩa pɛ ba lɛ aŋ bʋla pɩba, “Má kɛŋ doho a pʋŋa su tinteeŋ, má tɩɩ kʋŋ na-kala sɩ hɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ arɩ fille-la kala sɩ hɛ wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩɩ tuuri tinteeŋ.”
translated: The child said to his father, “If you give me a piece of meat, I will go and fetch water or I will sweep the compound or I will wash the dishes or I will pound millet.”

ref: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bee na kɩalɩŋ kala ʋ sɩ ŋaa dɩ ba zɔmɔ woruŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ wɩa jʋʋ dɩdaanɩŋ, taŋ pʋl chʋchʋɔŋ, chɛ-dʋmʋŋ nɛ ŋɩɩ.
translated: When God created the world, he made everything to be good. When God made human beings, he gave them wisdom, strength, and power.

ref: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa dɛrɛ ʋ tɩntɩŋŋaa aŋ wiesi chɛ-pɛmɩŋ.
translated: Today God has added another year to my life.

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
text: La TƖƖNA Wɩa joŋ baal-la hɛ ŋmaŋkaa-la tʋɔŋ nɛ Ɛdɩŋ tinteeŋ lɛ dɩ ʋ pɛrɛ aŋ bibeŋ ʋ nyuŋ.
translated: The chief went to the farm and there he met Edin sitting under a tree with his children eating their food.

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: ama ʋ bɩ maga dɩ ŋ dii tɩa-la nɛnɩŋ sɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ jɩŋ wʋzɔmʋŋ arɩ wʋbɔmʋŋ bʋa, dɩ ŋ nɛ die, ŋ jaŋ sʋʋ.”
translated: If you do not do good things for your friend today when you are strong and able, when you are weak, you will not get help, too.

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: Ŋ jaŋ kpɩɩnɛ suoluŋ aŋ na kʋdiilee halɩɩ a mʋ sʋʋ. Tɩɛrɩŋ nɛ mɩ kaa mɛŋ, ŋ jaŋ bɩra bɩrɩmɛ tɩɛrɩŋ.”
translated: If you want to get something good, you must work hard for it. Nothing comes to you if you just sit and wait.

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
text: Abɛl ma kɛŋ ʋ piesee dɔŋɔ sɩ lʋl pie-kee bumbuŋ a kpʋ a joŋ nammɩa-la lere-la sɩ zɔmɔ a ŋaa Wɩɩsʋlʋŋ. La TƖƖNA tʋɔŋ tɔrɛ Abɛl Wɩɩsʋlɩɩ-la nyuŋ,
translated: Abel and his siblings went to the farm and after working hard, they returned home and told their mother about what happened in Wiesule. The chief sent a message to Abel’s mother.

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: ama ʋ tʋɔŋ bɩ Keeŋ arɩ ʋ kɩaa nyuŋ tɔrɔ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Keeŋ faasa na baanɩŋ a kɛŋ sɩaa chei.
translated: My father went to Keeŋ and he bought a goat there. Now Keeŋ is looking for someone to help him bring the goat home.

ref: “This punishment is too hard!” Cain said.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Keeŋ bʋla pɩ La TƖƖNA Wɩa, “Ŋ dɔgɩsɛmɛ kieli nɛ.
translated: Then Keeŋ said to La TƖƖNA Wɩa, "I have finished talking."

ref: Then Enoch had a son named Irad, who had a son named Mehujael, who had a son named Methushael, who had a son named Lamech.
text: Ɛnɔk ma lʋl hambolibie a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Irad, Irad ma lʋl Mɛhʋjal, Mehujal ma nɛ lʋl Mɛtʋsal, Mɛtʋsal ma lʋl Lamɛk.
translated: Enoch gave birth to Irad, Irad gave birth to Mehujael, Mehujael gave birth to Methusael, Methusael gave birth to Lamech.

ref: Lamech married Adah, then Zillah.
text: Lamɛk jaa haalaa balɩa, Ada arɩ Zila.
translated: Lamach took two wives, Adah and Zillah.

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Dɩ nʋʋ nɛ kpʋ Keeŋ, dɩ ʋ nɩaa balɩpɛ sʋʋ ŋɩɩ nyuŋ lɛ, ŋɩɩ-na dɩ nʋʋ nɛ kpʋ mɩ-na Lamɛk, ʋ nɩaa mahɩŋ batori arɩ fii arɩ balɩpɛ jaŋ sʋʋ.”
translated: Two wives were taken by Keeŋ; the name of one was Adah, and the name of the other was Zillah. Adah gave birth to Jabal; he was the father of those who live in tents and raise livestock.

ref: and God took him away at the age of
text: Ɛnɔk to Wɩa wombiiŋ ŋɩɩ nɛ halɩɩ a mʋ tuo. Wɩa nɛ joŋu viiri.
translated: Enoch walked with God and then he was no more because God took him. God is powerful.

ref: Lamech said, “I'll name him Noah because he will give us comfort, as we struggle hard to make a living on this land that the Lord has put under a curse.”
text: Ʋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Nowa aŋ bʋl, “Wɩa fa ŋmɩɛsa hɛ tinteeŋ lɛ nɛ dɩ la tɩma suoli aŋ-na dii. Bie deeŋ nɛ jaŋ leŋ dɩ la tʋɔŋ pɩŋ dɩ-bala.”
translated: God said to Noah, “Take your whole family with you into the boat, because I have seen that you alone are righteous among all the people living on the earth. Take with you seven pairs, male and female, of each kind of clean animal.”

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa ŋaa wɩalɩŋ kala Wɩa sɩ sɩ ʋ ŋaa.
translated: God told Noah to build a big boat.

ref: Noah took a male and a female of every living creature with him,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ kʋŋ na-kala doho sɩ kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ, ba balɩa balɩa to Nowa harɩŋ a jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la.
translated: Just as it was in the days of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Son of Man.

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa bɩra gbɛrɛ tapʋlaa balɩpɛ, aŋ bɩra ta kokomo-la ʋ lɩɩ. Bʋa deeŋ-na lɛ kokomo-la bɩra bɩ mɩɩra jʋʋ ʋ teeŋ.
translated: When Noah finished building the big boat, he brought all kinds of animals into it. God told him to bring the animals in pairs into the boat.

ref: This includes the birds and the animals that came out of the boat.
text: a pɛ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩɩ wiesu lɛ: Zaaraa, bagɩlaa, pʋsʋŋ arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩ to ŋ harɩŋ lɩɩ lɩɩŋ daboro-la tʋɔŋ.
translated: There are three types of animals here: goats, sheep, and cows, and each of these animals has its own place in the pen.

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: mɩ jaŋ liisi nii-la mɩ sɩ bile mɩ dɩŋ pɛgɛ arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩ weye a ŋaa doho doho a hɛ dʋnɩa lɛ. Mɩ bɩra bɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ lɩɩ-balɩŋ kʋ chei kɩalɩŋ mɩɩsɩŋ kala.
translated: When I was young, I used to go to the farm with my father and we would work until the sun set. I remember those days with happiness because they were good times.

ref: All people on earth are descendants of Noah's three sons.
text: Nowa hambolibiisiŋ deemba batori nɛ kʋ ŋaa dʋnɩa nɩaa kala doho.
translated: The children are playing under the big tree near the house.

ref: As soon as Abram and Sarai arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians noticed how beautiful she was.
text: Ba sɩ kʋ jʋʋ Ijipiti tinteeŋ, Ijipiti tɩŋŋaa na dɩ Saraɩ ŋaa hazɔŋ nɛ.
translated: When they went down to Egypt, Egypt did not treat Sarah well because of her.

ref: Abram and Sarai took everything they owned and went to the Southern Desert. Lot went with them.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram arɩ ʋ haala arɩ kʋŋkala ʋ sɩ kɛnɛ lɩɩ Ijipiti tinteeŋ lɛ a mʋ Nɛgɛf tinteeŋ. Lɔt to ba harɩŋ.
translated: Abraham went from there toward the land of the Negev, between Egypt and the Negev. Lot went with him.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: Lee-la kala ŋ sɩ na, mɩ jaŋ joŋo pɩŋ, ŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa a kaa mʋ wuu.
translated: Long ago, when I was young, my father used to tell me many stories.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Jɩ-fii arɩ banɛsɩmɩŋ tʋɔŋ Kedolooma arɩ ʋ nɩaa yuo dii nɩaa deemba: Refem tɩŋŋaa sɩ hɔŋ Asɩtɛrɔf Kanam lɛ. Zuzi tɩŋŋaa sɩ hɛ Ham lɛ, Emi tɩŋŋaa sɩ hɔŋ Kɩrɩatam lɛ
translated: We went and gathered together at Kedolooma and we saw two people: Refem was sitting at Asiterof’s place. Zuzi was sitting at Ham’s place, Emi was sitting at Kiriatam’s place.

ref: At Siddim Valley, the armies of the kings of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, Zeboiim, and Bela fought the armies of King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Babylonia, and King Arioch of Ellasar. The valley
text: dɩ ba yuo Ɛlam kuoro Kedolooma, Goim kuoro Tɩdal, Sɩna kuoro Amɩrafɛl arɩ Ɛlasa kuoro Arɔk, kuoroo banɛsɛ a yuyuo kuoroo banɔŋ.
translated: They were at war: Elam’s king Kedolooma, Goiim’s king Tidal, Shinar’s king Amraphel, and Ellasar’s king Arioch—four kings against five.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ kuorusu-la banɛsɛ yuo dii Sodom arɩ Gomora nɩaa a laa ba kɩaa arɩ ba kʋdiilee kala a kaa viiri.
translated: If the people of Sodom and Gomorrah had seen the miracles that were done here, they would have repented and not perished.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Abɩram sɩ nɩa dɩ ba yuo a kɛŋ ʋ ŋaana bie Lɔt, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ yɩrɛ ʋ laalɩyuoroo zɔɔ-batori arɩ fii arɩ chori ba sɩ lʋla ʋ dɩa lɛ ʋ dagɛ ba laalɩŋ-yuonuŋ a ŋaa ba lɩɩ kiri nɩalɩŋ harɩŋ a mʋ yi taŋ-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Daŋ.
translated: Abraham and his family went with his nephew Lot, and they settled in a land where there were many people living, but after some time they separated because their possessions were too many for them to live together, so Lot chose the plain of Jordan and Abraham remained in Canaan.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ sʋl Wɩa pɩ Abɩram aŋ bʋl, “Wɩa-la sɩ kɩɩ kʋŋkala a ŋaa wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ tinteeŋ nɛ jaŋ pɛ Abɩram lɛ.
translated: God told Abraham, "God will show you a place where you and your descendants will live."

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: Mɩ bɩ ma kʋŋkala ka chɛ see kɩalɩŋ mɩ nɩaa sɩ die arɩ kpaal-la sɩ maga dɩ nɩalɩŋ sɩ to mɩ harɩŋ mʋ, Ana, Ɛsɩkɔl arɩ Mamɩrɛ, leŋ ba laa ba kpaalɩŋ.”
translated: When I was young and used to go to school, my mother would give me food to take with me, like Ana, Esikol and Mamire, so that I would not be hungry.

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: Wɩɩ-muluŋ sɩ tuu jʋʋ, doŋ faasa kɛŋ Abɩram. Ʋ nyuŋ ŋmɛrɛ, fawʋllʋŋ kɛnʋ woruŋ.
translated: We are all one people, just like Abraham. If we unite, nothing can defeat us.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ nii-la mɩ sɩ bile mɩ dɩŋ pɛgɛ chɩŋ a leŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa pʋŋa yʋgɛ kɩŋkɛŋ.”
translated: If I go there and they are not there, I will return and come and sit under this tree until they come back.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋla pɩ Wɩa, “Dɩ ŋ nɛ jaŋ pɛ Ɩsɩmɛl lɛ.”
translated: And Abraham said to God, “If only Ishmael might live under your blessing.”

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Ama mɩ ha jaŋ bil niiŋ mɩ dɩ Aɩzɩk, pɛgɛ, bile Sara sɩ jaŋ lʋl harɩŋ jɩna bʋa deeŋ lɛ a leŋ dɩ ʋ chɩŋ.”
translated: When I went to the farm with Aziz, we met Sara on the way and she told us to help her carry firewood to her house.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Bʋa deeŋ lɛ dɩ Abɩraham arɩ Sara hɩɛsa, Sara paala kieli biiriŋ lʋlʋŋ.
translated: God said to Abraham and Sarah, Sarah will give birth to a child.

ref: Sarah was so frightened that she lied and said, “I didn't laugh.” “Yes, you did!” he answered. Abraham Prays for Sodom
text: La TƖƖNA sɩ pɩɛsɛ ŋɩɩ, fawʋllʋŋ kɛŋ Sara, ʋ nyɩɛ wɩaa arɩ ʋ bɩ mɔmɔ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋl, “Ŋ mɔmɔ nɛ.” Abɩraham sʋla laa Sodom tɩŋŋaa
translated: The Lord appeared to him, as he was sitting near the tent, in the heat of the day, with his wife Sarah, and he saw three men standing nearby. When he saw them, he ran to meet them. The Lord said, "I will return." Abraham was living near Sodom.

ref: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Lɔt tolie-la balɩa kala ŋaa ba nyɩmma lɛ a kɛŋ luoruŋ.
translated: Lot’s wife looked back and she became a pillar of salt.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Ama Abɩmɛlɛk pɩŋ tɩtaŋɩŋ a duoso na Wɩa, ʋ bʋla pʋ, “Ŋ jaŋ sʋʋ, bɛɛ wɩaa haal-la ŋ sɩ leŋe ba kaa kʋ pɩŋ ŋaa nʋʋ haal nɛ.”
translated: When Abimelech was told about everything that had happened, he called them and said, “Why did you do this? All of you have brought trouble on me by doing what you did.”

ref: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.
text: Ʋ taŋ sɩ pʋla chʋchʋɔ-fɩɛla Abɩmɛlɛk sii yɩrɛ ʋ nɩhɩasɩŋ kala kʋ hilimi a bʋl wɩalɩŋ kala sɩ ŋaa a pɩba. Ba sɩ nɩɩ ŋɩɩ, fawʋllʋŋ kɛmba woruŋ.
translated: God came to Abimelech in a dream at night and said to him, "You are as good as dead because of the woman you have taken; she is a married woman."

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: Aɩzɩk sɩ yi tapʋlaa chori, Abɩraham keri ʋ peniŋ arɩ Wɩa fa sɩ dagʋ ŋɩɩ.
translated: When he was about to sacrifice his son, Abraham lifted up his knife and was about to kill him as God had told him.

ref: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Sara bʋla pɩ Abɩraham, “Kiri yomo-la arɩ ʋ bie ta, mɩ bɩ chɛ dɩ yomo-la bie pɛ mɩ bie Aɩzɩk lɛ dii dɩa.”
translated: So she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that slave woman and her son, for that woman’s son will never share in the inheritance with my son Isaac.”

ref: Early the next morning Abraham gave Hagar an animal skin full of water and some bread. Then he put the boy on her shoulder and sent them away. They wandered around in the desert near Beersheba,
text: Taŋ sɩ pʋla chʋchʋɔ-fɩɛla Abɩraham ŋaa kʋdiilee a ŋaa lɩɩŋ ma hɛ puruŋ tʋɔŋ a joŋo laalɛ Haga aŋ kiri ʋ dɩ ʋ bie ta. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba lɩɩ jaŋ-la lɛ a mʋ bɩbaarɛ Bɛɛsɩba tintee-hɩlɩɩ-la lɛ.
translated: So he sent away Abraham’s concubine Hagar and her child, and she left with her son. They lived in the wilderness of Beersheba.

ref: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.
text: Wɩa nɩɩ bile sɩɩ yel. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa tɩndaara lɩɩ wɩa-nyuŋ a yɩrɛ Haga a bʋl, “Bɛɛ nɛ, Haga? Sɩ́ fawʋllʋŋ fá, Wɩa nɩɩ bile sɩ pɩŋ nɩmɛ aa yel nɛ.
translated: The angel of the Lord spoke. He said to Hagar from heaven, “What is it, Hagar? Do not be afraid, the Lord has heard the boy crying where he is.”

ref: And so, Abraham promised he would.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋl, “Tɔɔ, mɩ jaŋ ŋmɩɛsɛ.”
translated: And Abraham said, "Yes, here I am."

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Ba sɩ bil nii-la Bɛɛsɩba lɛ dɛrɛ, Abɩmɛlɛk arɩ ʋ laalɩyuori-hɩaŋ Fiko mɩɩra mʋ Fɩlɩsɩtɩa tɩŋŋaa tinteeŋ.
translated: But in that year of famine, Abimelech took his family and went to live in the land of the Philistines at Gerar.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Abɩraham hɔŋ Fɩlɩsɩtɩa tɩŋŋaa tinteeŋ lɛ a dieni.
translated: Abraham stayed in the land of the Philistines for a long time.

ref: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
text: Ʋ sɩ mʋ yi taŋ-la dɩdaanɩŋ dɩɩrɩŋ, bʋa-la lɛ haalaa sɩ yie lɩɩ a ŋɩŋaa lɩɩŋ, ʋ leŋ bʋntaŋŋaba-la tuu pɩŋ viliŋ sɛmɛ.
translated: If you want to do good things, you must always think about how your actions will affect others.

ref: and I'll ask one of them for a drink. If she gives me a drink and then offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen and that you have kept your promise to my master.
text: Leŋ dɩ mɩ nɛ bʋla pɩ hantolibie, ‘Jaaŋ jaaŋ, mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, ŋaa lɩɩŋ pɩmɛ dɩ mɩ nyʋa,’ dɩ ʋ bʋl, ‘mɩ jaŋ ŋaa pɩŋ dɩ ŋ nyʋa aŋ pɛ ŋaa pɩ ŋ bʋntaŋŋaba maa,’ leŋ dɩ ʋ nɛ ŋaa nɩɩ-la ŋ sɩ lɩɩsa dɩ ŋ pɩ ŋ tɩntɩnna Aɩzɩk. Dɩ ŋ nɛ ŋaa ŋ-la, mɩ jaŋ jɩŋ arɩ ŋ dagɛ ŋ zile a pɩ mɩ kʋhɩaŋ nɛ.”
translated: Then he said to the hare, ‘Jaaŋ jaaŋ, my friend, come and help me,’ and the hare said, ‘I will come if you promise me that you will not betray me,’ then he took him and they went together to the house of Azik. When they arrived, he said, I will stand outside and watch while you go inside.”

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: Mɩ sɩ chʋɔlɛ Wɩa mɩ tʋɔŋ lɛ ŋ-la a dɛrɛ, dɩ Rɩbɛka kʋ lɩɩ a baasɛ ʋ nyuvie ʋ vaaŋ lɛ. Ʋ tuu lɩɩ-bulo a ŋaa lɩɩŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ bʋla pʋ, ‘Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, ŋaa lɩɩŋ pɩmɛ mɩ nyʋa.’
